message:
fix: Handle loop variable shadowing in inline for and PyValue iteration

When iterating over tuples with inline for (callable/heterogeneous) or
PyValue lists, the loop capture variable could shadow a previously
declared variable with the same name, causing Zig compilation errors.

Changes:
- For inline for with callable/heterogeneous tuples where var is already
  declared: use unique inner name (__inner_X_N) and temporarily remove
  from func_local_vars so rename takes effect
- For PyValue iteration: use unique capture name (__loop_X_N__) and
  assign to outer var (same pattern as regular for loops)

This fixes "capture 'x' shadows local variable from outer scope" errors.
